title: lophochloa hispida
intro: Lophochloa hispida, also known as the Hairy Bristle Grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Lophochloa hispida include treating fever, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the recovery from various illnesses. Herbal preparations of Lophochloa hispida include infusions, decoctions, and teas, which can be made by drying the plant's leaves and stems and then steeping them in hot water.
